(January 23, 1943 – December 16, 2025) was an American actor, whose roles include that of Captain [William "Buck" Rogers](/wiki/Buck_Rogers "Buck Rogers") in the 1979–1981 television series *[Buck Rogers in the 25th Century](/wiki/Buck_Rogers_in_the_25th_Century_(TV_series) "Buck Rogers in the 25th Century (TV series)")*.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American actor (1943–2025)

| Gil Gerard | |
| --- | --- |

| Spouses | * [Connie Sellecca](/wiki/Connie_Sellecca "Connie Sellecca")  ​  ​ (m. 1979; div. 1987)​ * Bobi Leonard  ​  ​ (m. 1987; div. 1989)​ * Janet Gerard |

**Gilbert Cyril Gerard** (January 23, 1943 – December 16, 2025) was an American actor, whose roles include that of Captain [William "Buck" Rogers](/wiki/Buck_Rogers "Buck Rogers") in the 1979–1981 television series *[Buck Rogers in the 25th Century](/wiki/Buck_Rogers_in_the_25th_Century_(TV_series) "Buck Rogers in the 25th Century (TV series)")*.

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gil_Gerard&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Gerard was born on January 23, 1943, in [Little Rock, Arkansas](/wiki/Little_Rock,_Arkansas "Little Rock, Arkansas"), as the youngest of three sons[[2]](#cite_note-2) to a college instructor mother and a salesman father.[[3]](#cite_note-People4.30.79-3) In 1960, he attended a [Maryknoll](/wiki/Maryknoll_Seminary "Maryknoll Seminary") seminary in [Glen Ellyn, Illinois](/wiki/Glen_Ellyn,_Illinois "Glen Ellyn, Illinois"), and played the title role in an all-male production of *[The Music Man](/wiki/The_Music_Man "The Music Man")*. He graduated from [Little Rock Catholic High School for Boys](/wiki/Catholic_High_School_for_Boys_(Little_Rock,_Arkansas) "Catholic High School for Boys (Little Rock, Arkansas)"),[[4]](#cite_note-4) and later attended the [University of Central Arkansas](/wiki/University_of_Central_Arkansas "University of Central Arkansas") but dropped out before graduation.[[3]](#cite_note-People4.30.79-3)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gil_Gerard&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

Gerard traveled to [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City"), where he studied drama by day and drove a [taxicab](/wiki/Taxicab "Taxicab") at night. Gerard picked up a fare who showed a lively interest in the problems of unknown, unemployed actors. Before he left the cab, he told Gerard to report in a few days to the set of *[Love Story](/wiki/Love_Story_(1970_film) "Love Story (1970 film)")*, which was being filmed on location in New York. When Gerard arrived on the *Love Story* set, he was hired as an extra. Later that day, he was singled out for a "bit" part, but he was not included in the finished film.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

During the next few years, he did most of his acting in [television commercials](/wiki/Television_advertisement "Television advertisement"), almost 400, including a stint as spokesman for the [Ford Motor Company](/wiki/Ford_Motor_Company "Ford Motor Company"). After small roles in the gay-themed film *[Some of My Best Friends Are...](/wiki/Some_of_My_Best_Friends_Are... "Some of My Best Friends Are...")* (1971), and the thriller *[Man on a Swing](/wiki/Man_on_a_Swing "Man on a Swing")* (1974), Gerard gained a prominent role in the daytime soap opera *[The Doctors](/wiki/The_Doctors_(1963_TV_series) "The Doctors (1963 TV series)")* for two years. Gerard formed his own production company in partnership with a writer-producer, co-authored a screenplay called *Hooch* (1977) and filmed it as a starring vehicle for himself. With *Hooch* completed, he traveled to [California](/wiki/California "California") to co-star with [Yvette Mimieux](/wiki/Yvette_Mimieux "Yvette Mimieux") in *Ransom for Alice!* (1977) and to play [Lee Grant](/wiki/Lee_Grant "Lee Grant")'s youthful lover in [Universal](/wiki/Universal_Pictures "Universal Pictures")'s *[Airport '77](/wiki/Airport_%2777 "Airport '77")* (1977). He appeared in a 1977 episode of *[Hawaii Five-O](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series)")* ("The Ninth Step") as Marty Cobb, a former cop and recovering alcoholic. A guest appearance in *[Little House on the Prairie](/wiki/Little_House_on_the_Prairie_(TV_series) "Little House on the Prairie (TV series)")* impressed producer-star [Michael Landon](/wiki/Michael_Landon "Michael Landon"), who cast him in the leading role in the 1978 [TV movie](/wiki/Television_movie "Television movie") *Killing Stone*.

Gerard then landed his best-known role, as Captain [William "Buck" Rogers](/wiki/Buck_Rogers "Buck Rogers") in the TV series *[Buck Rogers in the 25th Century](/wiki/Buck_Rogers_in_the_25th_Century_(TV_series) "Buck Rogers in the 25th Century (TV series)")*, which ran from 1979 to 1981, with the feature-length [pilot episode](/wiki/Buck_Rogers_in_the_25th_Century_(film) "Buck Rogers in the 25th Century (film)") being released theatrically some months prior to the first broadcast of the series. After this, he was featured in a number of other TV shows and movies, including starring roles in the 1982 TV movie *[Hear No Evil](/wiki/Hear_No_Evil_(1982_film) "Hear No Evil (1982 film)")* as Dragon,[[5]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTETerrace1985188-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTETerrace2011445-7) the short-lived series *[Sidekicks](/wiki/Sidekicks_(TV_series) "Sidekicks (TV series)")* (1986), and *[E.A.R.T.H. Force](/wiki/E.A.R.T.H._Force "E.A.R.T.H. Force")* (1990).[[1]](#cite_note-People10.15.90-1)

In 1992, Gerard hosted the [reality TV](/wiki/Reality_TV "Reality TV") series *Code 3*, which followed firefighters from different areas of the U.S. as they responded to emergency calls. The show ran on the [Fox TV](/wiki/Fox_Broadcasting_Company "Fox Broadcasting Company") Network until the following year. For the remainder of the 1990s, Gerard made guest appearances on various TV shows, including *[Fish Police](/wiki/Fish_Police_(TV_series) "Fish Police (TV series)")*, *[Brotherly Love](/wiki/Brotherly_Love_(1995_TV_series) "Brotherly Love (1995 TV series)")*, *[The Big Easy](/wiki/The_Big_Easy_(TV_series) "The Big Easy (TV series)")*, *[Days of Our Lives](/wiki/Days_of_Our_Lives "Days of Our Lives")*, and *[Pacific Blue](/wiki/Pacific_Blue_(TV_series) "Pacific Blue (TV series)")*.

In January 2007, Gerard was the subject of the one-hour documentary *Action Hero Makeover*, which was written, produced, and directed by his then-longtime companion, Adrienne Crow for the [Discovery Health Channel](/wiki/Discovery_Health_Channel "Discovery Health Channel"). The film documented his year-long progress after undergoing life-saving [mini-gastric bypass surgery](/wiki/Gastric_bypass_surgery#Mini-gastric_bypass_(MGB) "Gastric bypass surgery") in October 2005. According to the program, he had been struggling with his weight for 40 years, losing weight only to gain it back.[[8]](#cite_note-tgboc-8) By the time of the program's production, his weight had risen to over 350 pounds (159 kg), and he had many life-threatening health issues including a severe problem with [type 2 diabetes](/wiki/Diabetes_mellitus_type_2 "Diabetes mellitus type 2"). Within five days of the surgery he had lost 20 lb (9 kg), within three months he had lost 80 lb (36 kg), and within ten months he lost a total of 145 lb (66 kg).[[9]](#cite_note-9)

Gerard and his *Buck Rogers* co-star [Erin Gray](/wiki/Erin_Gray "Erin Gray") reunited in 2007 for the TV film *Nuclear Hurricane*, and also returned to the *Buck Rogers* universe by playing the characters' parents in the pilot episode of [James Cawley](/wiki/James_Cawley "James Cawley")'s *Buck Rogers Begins* Internet video series in 2009.[[10]](#cite_note-PressRepub-10) He guest-starred as Admiral Jack Sheehan in "Kitumba", the January 1, 2014, episode of the fan web series *[Star Trek: Phase II](/wiki/Star_Trek:_Phase_II_(fan_series) "Star Trek: Phase II (fan series)")*.[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12) In 2015, Gerard voiced Megatronus in *[Transformers: Robots in Disguise](/wiki/Transformers:_Robots_in_Disguise_(2015_TV_series) "Transformers: Robots in Disguise (2015 TV series)")*.

## Personal life and death

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gil_Gerard&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Personal life and death")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fd/Gil_Gerard_Mid_Ohio_Comic_Con_10222011.jpg/250px-Gil_Gerard_Mid_Ohio_Comic_Con_10222011.jpg)](/wiki/File:Gil_Gerard_Mid_Ohio_Comic_Con_10222011.jpg)

Gerard in 2011

By the end of the 1980s, Gerard had been married five times and divorced four times. His first marriage, in the 1960s to a secretary in his home state of Arkansas, lasted just eight months. After moving to New York to pursue his acting ambitions, his second marriage to a bank executive was equally troubled though it lasted (on and off) for seven years. Following his move to Los Angeles in the late 1970s, he married model/actress [Connie Sellecca](/wiki/Connie_Sellecca "Connie Sellecca") in 1979. Their son, Gilbert Vincent Gerard, or "Gib", was born in 1981 but the marriage began to disintegrate following the cancellation of Gerard's show *Buck Rogers in the 25th Century* and his increasing addictions to drugs, alcohol, and overeating. The marriage was formally dissolved in 1987, following a bitter custody battle which gave Sellecca main custody of their son. Gerard married again the same year, to interior designer Bobi Leonard, though the marriage lasted only a year and was then formally dissolved in 1989.[[1]](#cite_note-People10.15.90-1) His fifth wife was Janet Gerard, to whom he was married for 18 years until his death in 2025. The couple resided in northern [Georgia](/wiki/Georgia_(U.S._state) "Georgia (U.S. state)").[[13]](#cite_note-Janet1-13)

Gerard was frank about his battle with addictions. Although he went through recovery for his addiction to [cocaine](/wiki/Cocaine "Cocaine") and alcohol, following his divorce from Sellecca in the mid 1980s, his compulsive eating habits increased and he would find himself devouring unhealthy portions of [junk food](/wiki/Junk_food "Junk food"). By 1988, he weighed 300 lb (136 kg) and used a self-help treatment for his addiction, though he estimated that his weight problem had cost him work opportunities in the region of a million dollars. By 1990, he weighed 220 lb (100 kg).[[1]](#cite_note-People10.15.90-1)

On December 16, 2025, Gerard's wife, Janet, announced on [Facebook](/wiki/Facebook "Facebook") that he had died earlier that day following a "rare and viciously aggressive form of cancer". He was 82.[[13]](#cite_note-Janet1-13)
